event_summary: An Andes hantavirus outbreak aboard the Dutch-flagged cruise ship MV Hondius killed three passengers and infected at least ten people; the ship docked at Tenerife in mid-May 2026 for evacuation, with passengers quarantined across Europe, Australia, and Asia.
tldr: Headlines cluster tightly around quarantine logistics and case-count updates, with HEALTH_SAFETY dominating across 13 buckets. Argentina and Iran state media stand out with defensive national-innocence framings identical in structure but opposed in geopolitics. UK and US headlines break toward accountability, naming Trump's appointment and CDC notification failures — a POLITICAL rupture absent from every other bucket. Germany's headline asks whether hantavirus is comparable to Covid, anchoring PUBLIC_OPINION concern rather than case-count journalism.

isolation_top.note: Headline describes a first-person scientist narrative — form diverges from news-report titles across the corpus.
German-language explainer headline asking whether hantavirus compares to Covid; language and genre isolate it.
Korea Herald headline emphasised South Korea's historical role in hantavirus discovery — national-pride angle absent elsewhere.
Brief roundup headline naming quarantined nationals; low content limits semantic alignment with fuller corpus.
Single short RT headline quoting WHO chief; minimal title tokens reduce cosine match.

silences.what_they_covered_instead: First-person narrative headline from a scientist aboard the ship — genre departure from all other buckets.
Headline covered an unrelated Ebola item rather than the hantavirus outbreak.
Generic roundup headline noting quarantined nationals with no frame beyond logistics.

bottom_line: Headline framing is dominated by WHO-anchored case-count and quarantine logistics across 13 buckets, but the US and UK buckets break toward political accountability — the only regional press whose titles name governance actors rather than case numbers. China's complete headline absence from a global respiratory-pathogen story is the most significant silence.
